skill.name: claude-win11-speckit-update-skill
skill.description: Windows 11 system management guidance and patterns
skill.summary: Guidance for Windows 11 system management tasks
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 2 low-risk findings (GitHub URLs in metadata) and 2 false positive high-risk findings (non-existent cryptographic patterns). The skill contains no executable code, network operations, file system access, or security vulnerabilities. All findings were determined to be false positives. The skill is a minimal template with only documentation and metadata.

low_findings.title: GitHub URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md:4 and SKILL.md:22. These are legitimate GitHub repository links in skill metadata for source attribution. No network activity is performed by this skill.

content.value_statement: Get guidance for Windows 11 system management tasks. This skill provides patterns and best practices for Windows 11 administration using Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Provide guidance for Windows 11 system management
Offer patterns for system administration tasks
limitations: This is a guidance-only skill with no executable automation
Does not perform direct system operations or modifications
Requires manual implementation of provided patterns
Limited to documentation and advisory capabilities
No integration with Windows system APIs

use_cases.title: System Administration Planning
use_cases.description: Plan Windows 11 system management tasks with documented patterns and best practices

use_cases.title: Development Workflow Guidance
use_cases.description: Get guidance on Windows 11 development environment setup and management patterns

use_cases.title: Learning Windows Management
use_cases.description: Learn Windows 11 system management patterns and approaches through documented examples

prompt_templates.title: Basic System Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best practices for managing Windows 11 systems?
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Windows 11 system management

prompt_templates.title: Specific Task Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the recommended pattern for [specific Windows 11 management task]
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting guidance on a particular system management activity

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshooting Approach
prompt_templates.prompt: How should I approach troubleshooting [Windows 11 system issue] using standard patterns?
prompt_templates.scenario: Diagnosing and resolving Windows 11 system issues

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the advanced configuration patterns for [Windows 11 system component]?

output_examples.input: What are the best practices for managing Windows 11 systems?
output_examples.output: Here are key best practices for Windows 11 system management: Regular updates and patching, User access control management, System monitoring and maintenance, Backup and recovery planning, Security configuration hardening, Performance optimization, and Documentation of changes.

output_examples.input: Show me the recommended pattern for service configuration
output_examples.output: For Windows 11 service configuration, follow this pattern: 1) Identify service dependencies, 2) Configure startup type based on requirements, 3) Set appropriate service account permissions, 4) Document configuration changes, 5) Test in non-production environment first, 6) Monitor service health after deployment.

best_practices: Always test system management patterns in a non-production environment before production deployment
Document all system changes and maintain a change management log
Use Windows PowerShell or Windows Admin Center for automated system management tasks
anti_patterns: Making undocumented system changes without testing or rollback plans
Applying system management patterns without understanding their dependencies
Skipping backup and recovery procedures before system modifications

faq.question: What Windows 11 management tasks does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance and patterns for Windows 11 system management including configuration, administration, and maintenance tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill perform automated system operations?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and patterns only. It does not execute automated system operations or modifications.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my Windows 11 system settings?
faq.answer: No, this skill is documentation-only. You must manually implement any patterns or guidance it provides.

faq.question: What tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for Windows 11 system management guidance.

faq.question: Are the patterns provided specific to Windows 11?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns and guidance are specifically designed for Windows 11 system management.

faq.question: Do I need administrator privileges to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill itself requires no special privileges. However, implementing Windows 11 management patterns may require administrator access.
